title: Pfizer and Valneva report topline Phase 3 VALOR results and planned regulatory submissions
key_finding: Topline VALOR reporting states more than 70% efficacy in individuals aged 5+ and planned regulatory submissions; first pre-specified analysis did not meet the stated statistical criterion because fewer Lyme cases accrued than anticipated.
evidence_signal: Most advanced human Lyme vaccine candidate; regulatory pathway is the key near-term watch item.
actionability: Track FDA/EMA/other submissions, advisory committee materials, labeling, dosing schedule, and post-approval recommendation process.
tags: VALOR; efficacy; Phase 3; regulatory submissions; OspA; age 5+

title: CDC Lyme disease vaccine page: no current vaccine; LB6V completed Phase 3; ACIP would develop recommendations if approved
population: Public; healthcare providers; potential future vaccine populations not yet recommended
key_finding: CDC states that a Lyme disease vaccine is not currently available; it describes LB6V as having recently completed Phase 3 human trials and notes that ACIP would develop recommendations if FDA approval occurs.
evidence_signal: Public health recommendation work is contingent on regulatory approval.
actionability: Monitor CDC/ACIP agenda, FDA approvals, and public-facing education needs.
tags: CDC; ACIP; no current vaccine; public health guidance; LB6V

title: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for VALOR Phase 3 trial NCT05477524
population: Healthy children and adults in North America and Europe; age 5+ per sponsor reporting
key_finding: Registry record describes an efficacy, safety, tolerability, immunogenicity, and lot-consistency clinical trial of a 6-valent OspA-based Lyme vaccine for prevention of Lyme disease.
evidence_signal: Primary registry anchor for trial design and status.
actionability: Use registry fields for enrollment, sites, endpoints, arms, and last update in future automated refreshes.
tags: ClinicalTrials.gov; NCT05477524; VALOR; Phase 3; trial design

title: Valneva LB6V page summarizes Phase 3 efficacy signal and planned regulatory submissions
citation: Valneva R&D page, Lyme Disease — LB6V (VLA15)
population: Individuals aged 5 years and above per company summary
product_platform: Multivalent recombinant protein vaccine targeting six Borrelia OspA serotypes
key_finding: Valneva describes LB6V as the most advanced Lyme vaccine candidate and summarizes Phase 3 topline efficacy, tolerability, unmet statistical criterion in first analysis, and planned regulatory submissions.
evidence_signal: Company R&D page is a high-priority status source, but should be cross-checked against regulators.
actionability: Monitor press releases, filings, and regulator pages for confirmation of submission/approval milestones.
tags: Valneva; Pfizer; LB6V; VLA15; OspA; regulatory

title: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for mRNA-1975/mRNA-1982 Phase 1/2 trial NCT05975099
key_finding: Registry record describes evaluation of safety and immunogenicity of heptavalent mRNA-1975 and monovalent mRNA-1982 against Lyme disease in adult participants.
evidence_signal: Registry anchor for Moderna's initial human Lyme vaccine program.
actionability: Track posted results, peer-reviewed outputs, and links to later Phase 2 trials.

citation: Open Forum Infectious Diseases supplement abstract, 2026
population: Healthy adults 18 to 70 years of age
key_finding: Interim analysis abstract reported generally safe profiles with dose-dependent reactogenicity and immunogenicity after a 3-injection series, informing future trials.
evidence_signal: Early human safety/immunogenicity signal; detailed peer-reviewed publication/results posting still a watch item.

title: Blue Lake/CyanVac press release summarizes preclinical PIV5-based intranasal Lyme vaccine data
citation: Blue Lake Biotechnology press release, February 15, 2024
key_finding: Company release states that mice inoculated with PIV5-based intranasal Lyme vaccine candidates had long-lasting neutralizing antibody responses and protection up to 15 months against multiple Borrelia burgdorferi strains.
evidence_signal: Preclinical durability and intranasal route are differentiating attributes; human translatability unproven.
actionability: Monitor IND/Phase 1 announcements and safety data for PIV5-vectored intranasal platform in humans.
tags: PIV5; intranasal; Blue Lake; CyanVac; preclinical; durable response

developer: Blue Lake Biotechnology and CyanVac; Immuno Technologies collaboration
title: npj Vaccines article: intranasal PIV5 vaccine protected mice against tick-transmitted Borrelia beyond one year
key_finding: Peer-reviewed article reports longer-lasting antibody responses and protection in mice after intranasal PIV5-delivered OspA vaccination compared with parenteral recombinant OspA control in the tested model.
evidence_signal: Peer-reviewed preclinical support; next gap is clinical translation.
actionability: Track IND status, Phase 1 initiation, shedding/safety considerations, route acceptability, and manufacturing scalability.
tags: npj Vaccines; PIV5; OspA; mouse; intranasal; durability

title: Tufts Now summary: engineered CspZ target shows promise in preclinical Lyme vaccine development
population: Mouse studies; human immune-cell relevance noted in university summary
key_finding: University summary describes CspZ as a promising Lyme bacterial protein target and notes engineered variants producing robust immune responses in preclinical studies.
evidence_signal: Alternative non-OspA antigen strategy may diversify the field.
actionability: Monitor translational funding, manufacturing process, adjuvant/route decisions, and IND-enabling studies.
tags: Tufts; CspZ; recombinant protein; preclinical; alternative antigen

title: Nature Communications article on structure-based design of CspZ-targeting Lyme vaccine
key_finding: Study reports structure-guided mutations of CspZ-YA variants that improved protective activity in mouse challenge models and supported antibody-recognition/stability analyses.
evidence_signal: Mechanistic preclinical support for a non-OspA antigen approach.
actionability: Add manufacturing, adjuvant, toxicology, dose regimen, and translational readiness data when available.
tags: Nature Communications; CspZ; structure-guided design; mouse challenge; preclinical
